creator: yamagi@yamagi.org
text:
Just a "me too":

- /usr/local/share/vim/vimrc effects vim behavior, even if a ~/.vimrc is installed. But at least the user can work around it by setting 'set all&'.

- /usr/local/share/vim/gvimrc effects gvim behavior and a work around is hard. One can set an alias "alias gvim 'gvim -U ~/.vimrc'" but that's shell dependent and not suitable in all environments. Even worse, the default gvimrc breaks themes in interesting ways: http://deponie.yamagi.org/temp/kaputtervim.png

What about patching both system wide files with something like this:

let userconfig=expand("~/.vimrc")
if filereadable(userconfig)
	finish
endif
unlet userconfig

So users without ~/.vimrc would get the sample configuration and all others won't be effected by it.
